# fhir-capability-analyzer

> TypeScript CLI and library for fetching, analyzing, and comparing FHIR server CapabilityStatements.

`fhir-capability-analyzer` answers the question: "What does this FHIR server actually support?"
It fetches the CapabilityStatement from any FHIR server's /metadata endpoint, parses the deeply
nested JSON into an ergonomic data model, detects conformance to international profiles, generates
warnings for common configuration issues, and compares two servers' capabilities side by side.

## Core library (browser-safe)

```typescript
import { fetchCapabilityStatement, parseFromJson, analyze, compare } from "fhir-capability-analyzer";
import { detectProfiles } from "fhir-capability-analyzer/registry";
```

Key functions:
- `fetchCapabilityStatement(url)` — fetch + parse a CapabilityStatement from a FHIR server URL
- `parseFromJson(raw)` — parse a CapabilityStatement from an already-loaded JSON object
- `analyze(capability)` — produce an AnalysisReport (summary, profile conformance, warnings)
- `compare(a, b)` — produce a ComparisonReport (added, removed, changed differences)
- `detectProfiles(urls)` — detect known profile families from a list of profile URLs

## CLI

```bash
fhir-capability-analyzer analyze <url-or-file> [--format text|json|markdown]
fhir-capability-analyzer compare <sourceA> <sourceB> [--format text|json|markdown] [--exit-on-diff]
```

## Key types

- `ServerCapability` — ergonomic parsed CapabilityStatement
- `AnalysisReport` — { server, summary, conformance, warnings }
- `ComparisonReport` — { added, removed, changed } (ComparisonDifference[])
- `FetchResult` — discriminated union: { success: true, capability } | { success: false, error }
- `ProfileConformance` — { url, name, country, standard, version? }

## Profile detection

Detects: US Core, UK Core, AU Core/Base, CA Baseline, IPS, IPA, SMART App Launch, ISiK (Germany).
Detection is prefix-based on profile URLs declared in the CapabilityStatement.

## Output formats

- `text` — human-readable terminal output
- `json` — stable AnalysisReport/ComparisonReport JSON (for CI and AI agents)
- `markdown` — Markdown tables

## FHIR versions

R4 (4.0.1), R4B (4.3.0), R5 (5.0.0) — auto-detected from fhirVersion field.

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/fhir-capability-analyzer
- GitHub: https://github.com/dnlbox/fhir-capability-analyzer
- Docs: https://dnlbox.github.io/fhir-capability-analyzer/
- FHIR spec: https://hl7.org/fhir/
